page: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level virtual server belonging to the Developer family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1.0 GB of RAM, a 20 GB local SSD, and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates that this instance is positioned at the lower end of the performance spectrum. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yielded a score of 757.19, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, with read-only small block cached memory reaching 104.61 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds are 7.4 GB/sec and 12.75 GB/sec respectively. PassMark CPU testing scored 2078.63, and the PassMark CPU encryption test scored 1151.93, indicating weak cryptographic performance.

Due to the shared CPU allocation and limited 1.0 GB memory capacity, this server is designed for low-intensity workloads. It is highly cost-efficient for development environments, basic testing, lightweight scripting, or running simple static web servers, which achieved a throughput of 1.35 GB/sec in benchmarks. It is not suitable for resource-intensive databases, production applications, or heavy computational tasks.
description: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level virtual server in the Developer family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with a single shared vCPU powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 1.0 GB of RAM and 20 GB of local SSD storage. Designed for budget-conscious development and testing, its benchmark results place it in the lower performance tiers, with a stress-ng CPU score of 757.19 and a PassMark CPU score of 2078.63. Memory bandwidth is limited, reaching 7.4 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. This server is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, code prototyping, and running simple scripts where resource demands are minimal and cost efficiency is prioritized over high computational throughput.
og_description: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-1GB is a budget-focused developer server featuring a shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPU, 1 GB RAM, and 20 GB SSD storage, designed for lightweight testing and basic web serving.

tagline: A budget-friendly, entry-level virtual server with shared CPU resources and local SSD storage for basic development and lightweight testing workloads.

1.0 GB of system memory and 20 GB of local SSD storage
Includes one complimentary public IPv4 address
KVM hypervisor-based virtualization on x86_64 architecture
